entry.title: Re: Standard App/Report for Weekly Production
entry.content:
Dear @SaiBhargav396,

Option 1: Best standard match for your columns is Display Production Order Confirmation (CO14)

  • CO14 is a standard Public Cloud app (CO14) to display production order confirmations
  • In CO14 you can filter by a date range and use the operation overview where work-center related fields and confirmation quantities are shown

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/4304b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

How it maps to your requested report:

  • Work Center from the confirmed operation (operation overview)
  • Material Produced typically the production order material (header material)
  • Quantity Produced yield/confirmed quantity
  • Date Produced confirmation/posting date of the confirmation

So if the business accepts produced date = confirmation date, CO14 is the cleanest standard solution

SAP Notes below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002810926

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003425074

Option 2: If you’re in Repetitive Manufacturing use Display Production Quantities (MF51)

  • MF51 is a standard app to search/view/download production quantities for a plant/MRP area for a given period weekly works well

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/166bb6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('MF51')/S36

IMPORTANT: If you need Date Produced = Goods Receipt posting date

Then CO14 alone may not satisfy you because it’s confirmation-based. A GR-based list will give you material/qty/date but usually not work center. To get both GR date and work center in one weekly report the typical standard approach in Public Cloud is Embedded Analytics (Custom Analytical Query) combining the relevant released data sources

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/8573b810511948c8a99c0672abc159aa.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-analytical-queries-as-an-alternative-for-lis-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13700408

Summary:

  • Start with CO14 for a fast weekly production report export
  • If the business wants a true weekly dashboard/reporting experience build a Custom Analytical Query on the standard VDM content

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to schedule jobs to run every 30 minutes instead of every 3 hours with batch-based triggers
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support, 

 

In the Manage Situation Types - Extended Application, the selection for recurrence interval for batch based job scheduling is currently only 3, 6, 12, 24 hours, but we require the interval time of 30 minutes. 

Can this 'Repeat Interval' of 30 minutes be chosen. 

 

Thank you. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to schedule jobs to run every 30 minutes instead...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Thank you for your query regarding the recurrence interval in Manage Situation Types – Extended Application.

 

For batch-based job scheduling, the available repeat intervals for the 'Daily' repeat type are system-defined. The supported values are 3, 6, 12, and 24 hours.

 

Intervals shorter than 3 hours (for example, 30 minutes) are not supported. This limitation is in place to ensure system stability and prevent potential performance impacts.

 

You can find additional information on batch job scheduling in the following documentation:

Batch Job Scheduling. 

 

Kind regards, 

SAP Support

entry.title: SAP DSP – Automated Test Tool
entry.content:
Minor changes in sub-views can have huge impact on dependent Views (Model-Views/FACT-Level), Models, SAC Stories. Nothing new, but “Lernt Durch Schmerz” I found that even the approach: Test-Fail-Build-Test will help. Full regression testing is required as unexpected dependency can cause huge data issues. As with all projects, time pressure may not allow full regression testing and a single developer, nor the Product Owner, will know the entire system / impact of the requested changes.

Testing by the end-user should be the last Option.

Development in SAC is more application oriented and as of now I am now aware of a way to fully test SAC Stories from functional and data side. Alternative would be to use SAC Excel-Addin to download snapshots of AMs and compare before and after the changes were made. For this alternative VBA scripting would be required and I am not sure if fully automated Excel / Workbook based Test can be done easily. Therefore, here is a proposal for DSP based Automated Test Tool supporting CD/CI approach.

Objects Used:

Type

Usage

Table

Snapshot table

Table View (private-like/base)

Dimension view based on the Snapshot table

Model-View (query-like/consumption)

FACT-Level View consuming the Table view

Model

Model used in the Story

FACT-Level View (query-like/consumption)

Object to be regression tested

KPI Generator View

Snapshot generator for each Object to be regression tested

Data Flow

Pushing Snapshots to Snapshot table

Task Chain

Executing the Data Flow/s

Story

Evaluating the data

 

How-To:

We have a limitation in DSP, that only Views can be used in Data Flows. Therefore only base-line Measures can be used as of now. Base-line Measures test will catch 80-90% of issues, however if this limitation is a problem, an alternative would be to use OData on Models and use Azure f.E. as base for the tool.

1. Create the Snapshot table: I used Provider (I am from BW world), Date, Time, Version, KPI and Value. I omitted Unit as I select the Unit in the KPI Generator View.

2. Define the KPIs to be regression tested: selection on these KPIs should be in the past (last year f.e.) ensuring stable data. Most critical / most used base-measures should be selected as KPIs. Even Row-Count can be considered in case master data is changed (f.e. time-dependency is introduced).

3. Create the KPI Generator View: based on the chosen KPIs simply aggregate the results with dedicated KPI naming (f.E. NetRevenue, ...).

4. Create Data Flow: which will just push the Snapshots into the Snapshots table.

5. Create Taks Chain, Model, Story: for easier consumption / evaluation of the snapshots. Alternatives can be using max and have two Snapshot tables (one for before and one for after, union both and consume via the Story).

Approach on DEV System (optional):  

  1. Create Snapshots via Task Chain scheduled every morning / evening
  2. Have the developers check on their own

Approach on TST System:

  1. Create Snapshots via Task Chain run before the transport
  2. Import all objects
  3. Create Snapshots via Task Chain run after the transport
  4. Compare before and after the transport
  5. All differences must be explainable

 

Snapshot table:

Field

Usage

PROVIDER

FACT-Level View / Tested Object unique ID

DATE

Snapshot date

TIME

Snapshot time

VERSION

Usage (optional)

KPI

The KPI to be tested

Value

Value

 

Dimension view based on the Snapshot table:

SELECT * FROM [SNAPSHOT TABLE]

Optional. I never directly use tables. Learned that from my BW experience developing Extractors on the ECC side.

 

FACT-Level View consuming the Table view:

In our case SELECT * FROM [SNAPSHOT VIEW], but FACT-Level to be used as Model-View. Can be enhanced with Associations f.E.

 

Model used in the Story:

To be used in the Story for easier evaluation.

 

Snapshot generator for each Object to be regression tested:

In this view, the KPIs and Provider are defined. Basically the most-used / most-critical base-measures. For each object tested, one Snapshot generator view should be created. Alternatively one view could be created, but having hundreds of SQL lines is not the best approach.

This is just an example view:

 

Data Flow Pushing Snapshots to Snapshot table:

 

Task Chain:

Just executing the Data Flow/s

 

Story:

 

Basically comparing Snapshots before and after. Basic testing approach, just allowing to be automated and covering all critical Models.

Main blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/bw-vs-datasphere-dsp-amp-sac/ba-p/1428...

entry.title: Re: Can we create our own dpi in the sustainability contr...
entry.content:
Hi Narendra, I hope you're doing great! 

I am Kauê from Sustainability SCT/SFM support team! 

In SAP Sustainability Control Tower, you cannot create a completely new DPI object with its own ID. The product provides a fixed set of DPIs, and all data imports must follow one of these structures.

Instead, extend existing DPIs:

  • Create custom dimensions via “Manage Custom Dimensions” app and assign to DPIs.​

  • Create custom measures via “Manage Measures” linked to those DPIs/dimensions.

  • Build custom metrics (your KPIs) on top via “Manage Metrics”.​

This allows customer‑specific data models without new DPI types.

Official SAP Help Page SCT docs:

  • #Data Provider Interfaces (DPIs).

  • #Manage Custom Dimensions.

  • #ESG Data Model Basics.

entry.title: Re: Discount on invoices
entry.content:
Hello @Vir4-Ji_ ,

There is no direct KZBTR-equivalent indicator field in ACDOCA.
Discount identification in S/4HANA must be done via:

-Cash discount amount fields (SKNTO etc.)
-Payment terms (ZTERM)
-Discount G/L account postings
-Clearing document references

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Technical Fields & SAP Notes
In BSEG I can see field KZBTR (cash discount indicator), but not in ACDOCACorrect. In S/4HANA, BSEG is no longer the leading source for reporting. The Universal Journal (ACDOCA) replaces many classical fields, and some BSEG indicators are not directly replicated as standalone flags.
Is there a direct “discount indicator” field in ACDOCA?❌ No single equivalent field like KZBTR exists in ACDOCA. Discounts are reflected through amounts and condition logic, not through a simple indicator field.
How are cash discounts represented in ACDOCA?Cash discounts are reflected through: • Fields: SKNTO (Cash Discount Amount in Document Currency) • Separate line items posted to discount accounts (e.g., revenue reduction or expense reduction). • Clearing information (REBZG, AUGBL, AUGDT).
How to identify if an FI invoice includes cash discount terms?Check: • Payment terms (ZTERM) in ACDOCA/BKPF context • Baseline date (ZFBDT) • Discount amounts SKNTO / SKNT2 / SKNT3 fields (depending on level).
How are logistics invoice (MM) discounts represented?Logistics discounts (pricing conditions) are usually reflected as: • Reduced invoice amount at posting time • Separate condition-based line items if configured They are not stored as a simple indicator in ACDOCA.
Can I identify discount by G/L account?✅ Yes (recommended method). Identify discount accounts (e.g., cash discount revenue/expense accounts). If document contains posting to those G/L accounts, discount was applied.
How to detect statistically in reporting?Use combination of: • ACDOCA-SKNTO (cash discount amount) • ACDOCA-HKONT (discount G/L account) • Clearing fields (AUGBL) to detect discount realized at payment.
Why no direct KZBTR equivalent?S/4HANA redesign removed redundant indicators. Universal Journal stores actual values and accounting impact rather than flags. Reporting should rely on amounts and account postings.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Universal Journal Entry (ACDOCA) SAP Help → Cash Discount Posting Logic in Accounts Payable / Receivable
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Version Applicability)📌 SAP Note 2156822 – Universal Journal Field Mapping (BSEG to ACDOCA) 📌 SAP Note 2349297 – Cash Discount Handling in S/4HANA Finance 📌 SAP Note 2431747 – Reporting Differences Between BSEG and ACDOCA 📌 SAP Note 1976487 – Cash Discount Posting Logic in FI

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Manual Rework activity appearing in production Manage...
entry.content:
Hello @R_Pooja ,

Manual Rework tasks are system-specific and triggered by upgrade consistency checks.
Differences between Q and P data can cause the task to appear only in Production.
Even if master data is already maintained, you must review and confirm the rework activity to close it.

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Required Action & SAP Notes
Manual Rework activity appears after 2602 upgradeDuring major releases (e.g., 2602), SAP delivers configuration or master data consistency checks. If Production data does not fully align with new logic, a Manual Rework task is triggered automatically.
Why only in Production and not in Quality?In 2SL landscape, Q and P systems may have slight master data or configuration differences at upgrade time. The rework check runs system-specifically. If Production had documents, transactional data, or historical setup not present in Q, the check may trigger only in Production.
Master data already maintained, but still showing Action RequiredThe rework check validates specific technical attributes (e.g., new fields, flags, consistency rules). Even if business data exists, the new required attribute might be missing or not migrated automatically.
Is action required if data seems correct?✅ Yes, you must open the rework task and validate each item. Even if data appears maintained, SAP requires confirmation to mark the rework as completed. Otherwise, the task remains open.
Could it be a false positive?Occasionally, yes. Some upgrade checks are triggered based on system conditions. If data is already compliant, you can confirm and complete the rework activity manually.
Why triggered after 2602 specifically?Release 2602 likely introduced new validation logic or mandatory fields in configuration/master data. Production-specific data inconsistencies can trigger rework tasks post-upgrade.
What happens if no action is taken?System remains operational, but the rework task stays open and may cause audit/compliance warnings during future upgrades. Best practice is to review and complete it.
Recommended Action1️⃣ Open the rework activity in Manage Your Solution 2️⃣ Review impacted objects 3️⃣ Validate master data attributes 4️⃣ Confirm completion in app
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Upgrade and Release Management in S/4HANA Cloud SAP Help → Manual Rework Activities After Upgrade
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Applicability for 2602 Release)📌 SAP Note 3116400 – Manual Rework Activities After Upgrade in S/4HANA Cloud 📌 SAP Note 2831001 – Upgrade-Related Configuration Consistency Checks 📌 SAP Note 3090106 – Release Upgrade Impact in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Budget exceeded error BP603, WBS element - PS Module
entry.content:
Hello @cciol ,

Check where budget is assigned (project vs WBS level).
Ensure budget is distributed to the sender WBS.
Review control profile (OKOB) for hierarchical check settings.
Confirm value categories in allocation structure match the budget category.

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Root Cause & SAP Notes
WBS element uses another WBS (same project) as allocation senderIn PS, budget is controlled at WBS level according to Availability Control (AVC) settings. Even within the same project, each WBS has its own budget and assigned value categories.
BP603 error occurs even though budget seems availableBP603 is triggered because the sender WBS does not have sufficient available budget in the relevant value category at the time of allocation. The system checks the sender WBS individually, not the overall project total.
Exceeded amount equals allocation valueThis indicates the sender WBS has zero available budget in the controlled value category. The system cannot “pull” budget from another WBS automatically within the same project.
Why does it work when using WBS from another project and disabling AVC (CJBW)?Disabling Availability Control removes the budget check, so allocation posts successfully. This confirms the issue is AVC logic, not allocation configuration.
Why same-project WBS behaves differentlyWithin the same project, budget distribution and control profile may restrict budget usage to the specific WBS element. If budget is assigned at higher level (e.g., project definition or parent WBS) and not distributed downward, child WBS cannot consume it.
What to check✔ Budget distribution (CJBU / budget supplement) ✔ Control profile in OKOB ✔ Assigned value categories ✔ Budget tolerance limits ✔ Whether budget is assigned at parent but not released to child
Key Technical LogicAvailability Control checks table BPJA (budget) vs actuals/commitments in COSP/COSS. It evaluates WBS element individually unless control profile allows hierarchical consumption.
Recommended FixOption 1: Distribute budget to sender WBS. Option 2: Adjust control profile to allow hierarchical availability check. Option 3: Review value category assignment in allocation structure (OKO6).
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Project System → Budget Management → Availability Control SAP Help → Budget Distribution in WBS Elements
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Version Applicability)📌 SAP Note 360216 – BP603 Budget Exceeded Message Explanation 📌 SAP Note 548013 – Availability Control Logic in Project System 📌 SAP Note 175784 – Budget Distribution and Hierarchical Control 📌 SAP Note 1606807 – Budget Availability Issues in Internal Allocations


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Invoice delivery audit
entry.content:
Hi,

In sap business one our current workflow is to create an A/R invoice+payment then give the customer his purchased items, some customers don’t pick their order the same day which makes the actual stock in the warehouse different than that on SBO.

Is it possible to make the delivery after the invoice I tried using A/R Reserve invoice which the workflow I need but in the same time it doesn’t decrease the stock which may cause two customers buying the same quantity in the warehouse.

In short I want to use the delivery as audit only, with no actual effect on the system.

Any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: Running SAP AMS Like a Product, Not a Support Factory
entry.content:
Very well articulated. The point about “optimizing for closure, not stability” really stands out.

 

I have seen similar patterns in AMS where SLA dashboards look green, but repeat incidents during critical cycles like month-end erode business confidence.

 

Measuring recurrence at process level instead of only response time is a powerful shift. Strong reminder that stability is an outcome, not an activity.

entry.title: CDS-Based OData 2.0 – Power BI Service Returns Status Code 400 (Invalid Credentials) for Production
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are experiencing an issue with a CDS-based OData service (auto-published via @odata.publish: true) when connecting from Power BI Service.

System & Technical Info:

  • OData Version: 2.0

  • Authentication Type: Basic Authentication

  • Same User ID used in both clients

  • User authorizations confirmed identical (Full Access)

Issue Details:

  • Client 300 (Production) – Power BI Service: Fails with “Failed to update data source credentials: The credentials provided for the OData source are invalid. Status Code: 400.”

  • Client 300 – Power BI Desktop: Works without issues

  • Client 180 (Development) – Power BI Service & Desktop: Works without issues

  • OData URLs (Client 180 & 300): Both accessible and responsive

Checks Performed:

  • No authorization errors in SU53

  • No relevant errors in /IWFND/ERROR_LOG

  • Roles and authorizations between clients verified identical

Question:
Has anyone encountered a situation where a CDS-based OData service works in Power BI Desktop, works in Power BI Service for one client, but fails in Power BI Service for another client with Status Code 400?

Which additional checks would you recommend for:

  • CDS OData activation consistency

  • /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE registration

  • SICF node configuration differences

  • Client-specific security or profile parameters

  • Basic Authentication handling in Power BI Service

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Rap: Excel Upload and Download Excel using Rap only
entry.content:
@signortintin if you're facing issue then you can remove the

acceptableMimeTypes restrictions in below and use.

@Semantics.largeObject : {
      mimeType: 'Mimetype',
      fileName: 'Filename',
      acceptableMimeTypes: [ 'application/vnd.ms excel',
     'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'],
      contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT
      }

This will allow you to select any type of mime type.

So the annotations would look like 

@Semantics.largeObject : {
      mimeType: 'Mimetype',
      fileName: 'Filename',
      contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT
      }

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Rap: Excel Upload and Download Excel using Rap only
entry.content:
@marcelloganzaroli  by using this piece of code , I was able to place button on header ie in object page.

identification: [{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'Upload Data', dataAction: 'uploadExcelData' },
              { type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'Download Excel', dataAction: 'DownloadExcel' },

entry.title: Re: Edit Date via Date Action
entry.content:
Hi @andreag412 ,

you can try the below to generate the required data using data action. I am assuming the requirement is to copy the amount from billing date to payment date using the payment day measure and initial data is saved with no payment date.

Try using the below script to achieve the desired result.

INTEGER @DATE
FOREACH [d/Billing_Date]
@DATE = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Paymnet_Day",[d/Payment_Date]="2026-01-01",[d/Audit]="Old")
DATA([d/Measures] = "Amount",[d/Payment_Date] = NEXT(@DATE,"DAY",[d/Billing_Date]),[d/Audit]="New") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Amount") 
ENDFOR

BEFORE

AFTER

Thanks

Shivam

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Regulatory Outlook 2026 and Onwards
entry.content:
A few weeks ago, the OECD released its Regulatory compliance costs and productivity: new task-based evidence and its findings are unequivocal: the size and complexity of overall regulation have increased in OECD countries.

In addition to circumstantial evidence, the study also based its conclusions based on an estimated share of labour resources devoted to regulation-related tasks. As per the report, in the USA “the wage share devoted to compliance tasks increases from 4.0% in 2012 to 4.2% in 2024, while remaining stable at about 4.5% in Australia. In Europe, an employment-based indicator rises from 3.7% in 2011 to 3.9% in 2023”.

In summary, this means that over 4% of aggregate wages are spent on regulatory compliance tasks in the US and Australia and nearly that figure in Europe.

Illustrating this inflationary trend, the authors cite a few examples such as:  

  • The total number of restrictions in the US Code of Federal Regulations went from 400 thousand restrictive words in 1970 to more than 1 million today
  • In the EU, the number of Commission legislative proposals has increased from 374 during 1999- 2004 to 431 during 2019-2024 while the average length of each proposal has almost doubled and their language has also become increasingly complex

These trends show that there was a parallel evolution in those various geographies. At least until recently.

Indeed, whereas the previous decades had been marked with some level of compliance harmonization across the globe where internal control, codes of conduct or financial transparency requirements had been enforced in many countries with great similarities, it seems the world is at a fracture point with strong divergences between major economic blocks.

In a striking opposition, the European Union continues on its “Brussels effect” trajectory with the idea that regulations can better protect consumers and market competitiveness, whereas there is a perceived rollback of the US on compliance enforcements and even a move towards de-regulation with the idea that less business constraints is overall more beneficiary.

For organizations operating globally, this new reality will have to be considered for sure, but, if the US potentially slows down its regulatory inflation, it’s undeniable that the European Union will follow but other major countries such as China, Australia, Singapore and others will not. And there are various areas in which regulatory pressure will continue to increase.

In this blog, I have tried to summarize the findings of 30+ studies to identify the key compliance areas that will require attention from 2026 onwards due to new or more stringent mandates:

As of last year already, Cybersecurity is top of mind, but it is this year very much intertwined with a more all-encompassing digital resilience perspective.

Legislation on digital (or cyber) resilience set minimum requirements on how an organization must prepare for, withstand, and recover from disruptions that affect its digital systems, data and services, including cyberattacks, outages, and third‑party failures.

An example here would be EU’s Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA).

It is to be noted that a recent “subcategory” is being introduced here: Data Use and Access Acts that regulate the sharing of data generated by IoT devices and industrial machines - including non-personal data.

When it comes to AI governance, regulations are designed to establish rules for the ethical development, deployment, and oversight of AI systems to mitigate risks like bias, privacy violations, and lack of transparency. A key intent is to create an accountability for the AI models delivered – especially when used in customer context (i.e. either to deliver a service or make an informed decision on whether to grant a service to a customer) and to ensure human oversight in critical decisions.

The EU Artificial Intelligence Act is the world's first comprehensive AI law, but the United States has also decided for similar legislation – albeit decentralized – with the Colorado's AI Act or the California AI Transparency and Employment Laws for example.

Third-party and supply chain regulations mandate organizations to identify, assess, monitor, and mitigate operational risks from their vendors and suppliers, but also their extended fourth parties, to prevent disruptions, data breaches, or compliance failures.

Some of the regulations in this category can be cybersecurity focused – such as NIS2 Directive for instance, others will be very much in the “physical” world such as the Modern Slavery Act in the UK, the Duty of Vigilance Law in France or the Supply Chain Due Diligence Act in Germany.

For years, digital asset markets operated in a regulatory grey zone. Digital and payments regulations are meant to address this void and govern electronic transactions, data flows, and financial innovations such instant payments, open banking, and digital identities to ensure security, competition, and consumer protection.

Requirements related to Payment Services Directive 3 in Europe focused on fraud prevention, consumer rights and innovation is illustrative of this category focused on digital services.

Some frameworks have also been specifically focused on certain digital asset types such as the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation regulating crypto-assets not covered by traditional financial laws.

Legislative requirements in this category are meant to ensure that companies operate responsibly and sustainably. As a result, these standards are designed to measure a company's impact on the environment (climate change, natural resource and waste management), its social responsibilities (diversity and inclusion, protection of human rights), and its governance practices (board diversity, compensation model).

The International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), Sustainability Disclosure Standards, Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) standards or the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) are good illustrations of associated mandates.

For many institutions, ESG reporting remains isolated, disconnected from the systems that manage risk and compliance. With the mandates mentioned just above, regulators are closing that gap, expecting firms to show how their sustainability performance is linked to governance, strategy, and accountability.

If Digital Assets and Payments regulations already included a focus on fraud detection and deterrence, financial crime regulations are legal frameworks designed to prevent, detect, and report all illicit activities such as money laundering, terrorist financing, sanctions evasion, bribery, and proliferation financing.

Requirements in this category are multiform from due diligence mandate to transaction monitoring, suspicious activity reporting, risk assessments, and record-keeping as a basis for all.

In addition to Anti-Money Laundering laws such as the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Act in Australia or the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act in Malaysia, there are of course also the numerous sanctions lists: Denied Persons List, Non-proliferation Sanctions, Consolidated List, List of Statutorily Debarred Parties, Military End User List, OFAC Non-SDN Sanctions, Specially Designated Nationals List, Unverified List, Consolidated United Nations Security Council Sanctions List of Individuals and Entities… to name but a few!

Regulations in this last category set minimum behavioral standards by promoting personal responsibility, clear governance, and proper conduct standards to act with due integrity, reduce consumer harm, and enhance market integrity. The main requirements relate to taking reasonable steps to identify and manage conflicts of interest – especially for employees looking after customer interests or executives with roles of authority (Senior Managers). As its name suggests, the Senior Manager Regime in the UK is a representative of this matter.

What about you, what are the main regulatory requirements on your radar for the year(s) to come? I look forward to reading your thoughts and comments on this blog.

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Re: Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting Specific - d...
entry.content:
Good day @djwafelap 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, this can happen in Public Cloud when the tax depreciation area (for example, area 15) is not deployed or not shown because you did not fulfil its accounting principle and ledger assignment prerequisites in your tenant. So, basically depreciation areas such as 15 Local Tax are shown only when the local principle and ledger assignments are correctly maintained for the company code.

Reference the expert Blog for good guidance: Asset Accounting – Configuration Guide for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

If the depreciation area is missing in your tenant (even after you have imported transports), the first place you should validate is Assign Accounting Principle to Ledgers and Company Codes because missing depreciation areas in valuation views can occur when this prerequisite is incomplete or inconsistent.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/3781c5d2efea4956b4896d728916ad78.html?locale=en-US

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3530183

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3696205

After you have maintained the accounting principle assignment, use the make company code settings- Asset Accounting -Specific app to re check under ledger 2L à Depreciation Area Settings and you should be able to see the missing area.

If after that it still does not appear, then troubleshoot with KBA 3696205 and carry out another configuration through your CBC by re saving and transporting the configuration required.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Joule Studio: Documentation for Document Grounding and Testing, Deploying & Launch Joule Agent
entry.content:
Note: Enablement of Joule Studio (IMPORTANT): Joule Studio Enablement Guide

📑 Table of Contents:

  1. Introduction
  2. What Is Document Grounding in Joule Studio?
  3. Architecture Overview
  4. Prerequisites
      SAP Services Requirements
      Access Requirements
      Required Credentials from Service Keys
  5. Setting Up Destinations
      AI Core Destination
      Object Store Destination
  6. Configuring Document Grounding in Control Tower
      Create Resource Group
  7. Monitoring Status
  8. AWS S3 CLI Guide for Uploading Documents for Grounding
  9. Viewing Grounding Pipelines in SAP AI Launchpad
  10. Adding Document Grounding to Joule Studio Agents
  11. Using Joule Agent - Test in Private Environment and Deploy & Launch Joule Agent
  12. Troubleshooting

1. Introduction

Document Grounding is a capability in Joule Studio that lets you attach custom documents (PDFs, manuals, SOPs, policies, knowledge bases) to an AI agent.
This ensures the agent gives highly accurate, contextually relevant, and source-backed answers.

In older setups, admins had to manually manage:

  • Generic Secrets
  • Base64 encoding
  • Pipelines in SAP AI Launchpad

Now, Joule Studio automates these tasks through Control Tower, simplifying the job for developers.

 

2. What Is Document Grounding in Joule Studio?

Document Grounding performs these functions:

  1. Reads documents stored in an S3-compatible Object Store
  2. Creates a grounding pipeline inside SAP AI Core
  3. Creates a Resource Group to store the pipeline + embeddings
  4. Makes the resource available to Joule Studio Agents

This allows the agent to:

  • Retrieve answers strictly from the grounded documents
  • Reduce hallucinations
  • Use enterprise-verified content

3. Architecture Overview

Key interactions:

  • Control Tower creates Resource Groups
  • AI Core runs embedding pipelines
  • Object Store provides documents
  • Joule Studio binds the grounding data to agents

 

4. Prerequisites

You must have:

SAP Services

  • SAP AI Core (INSTANCE - Extended Plan)
  • SAP AI Launchpad (APPLICATION - Standard Plan)
  • Object Store (INSTANCE - s3-standard or standard)
  • SAP Build Control Tower Access 
  • SAP Build Lobby with Joule Studio enabled (Joule Studio Enablement Guide)
  • Add all the roles to the user from BTP Cockpit → Security → User → (select the user(s)) → Role Collections → Assign Role Collections → Select all the roles related to that (SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, Object Store, and SAP Build )

NOTE: For Object Store plan Standard, make 2 keys with Role "Read" and "Write".

 



Access Requirements 

  • Admin role for Control Tower
  • Developer role for Joule Studio
  • Object Store admin or contributor
  • AI Core admin or contributor

Required Credentials from Service Keys

  • AI Core Service Key
  • Object Store Service Key

5. Setting Up Destinations

Two destinations must be configured:
1. AI Core Destination
2. Object Store Destination
These allow Joule Studio to perform grounding operations behind the scenes.

AI Core Destination

Step 1 — Retrieve AI Core Service Key

From BTP → Instance → Service Key

Important fields:

  • url
  • clientid
  • clientsecret
  • tokenurl (must modify)

Step 2 — Configure Destination

Destination Type: HTTP

Field

Value

URL

<AI Core Service Key URL>

Client ID

clientid

Client Secret

clientsecret

Token Service URL

<AI Core URL>/oauth/token (must append /oauth/token)

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

Step 3 — Add Additional Properties

These ensure visibility in Joule Studio.

Property

Value

sap-joule-studio-document-grounding-ai-core

 true

Important Note

If you forget to append /oauth/token, the grounding pipeline will not trigger.

 

Object Store Destination

Step 1 — Retrieve Object Store Service Key


Note: For Standard Plan, Use the Read role key for the destination.

Extract values such as:

  • access_key_id
  • secret_access_key
  • host
  • bucket
  • region

Step 2 — Configure Destination

Destination Field

Service Key Field

URL

host (add https://)

Client ID

access_key_id

Client Secret

secret_access_key

Token Service URL

host (add https://)

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

Proxy Type

Internet

Step 3 — Additional Properties

Property

Value

region

region from service key

bucket

bucket value

username

username from service key

type

S3

sap-joule-studio-document-grounding-object-store

true

These properties ensure that Joule Studio can detect the Object Store.

 

 

6. Configuring Document Grounding in Control Tower

Control Tower now includes a Joule module with Document Grounding options.

Admins decide:

  • Which Object Store to use
  • Which AI Core instance will create pipelines

6.1 Create Resource Group

Steps:

  1. Go to SAP Build Control Tower
  2. Navigate:
    Control Tower → Joule → Document Grounding
  3. Click Add
  4. Provide a Resource Group Name
  5. Select the AI Core Destination
  6. Select the Object Store Destination
  7. Click Create

A new resource group is created and grounding begins automatically.

 

7. Monitoring Status

Resource group states explained:

Status

Meaning

Pending

Pipeline running; embeddings not finished

Completed

Successfully grounded

Error

Incorrect destination or corrupted document

Troubleshooting:

  • Check destination credentials
  • Ensure documents are not corrupted
  • Ensure Object Store bucket name matches

Note:

After approximately few minute (depending on the size of the documents), the status will change to Completed.

In earlier instances, error messages occasionally appeared, or the status remained Pending for an extended period. At times, the status alternated between these messages. In such cases, it is important to ensure that the destinations are configured correctly and that the documents in the Object Store are not corrupted.

 

8. AWS S3 CLI Guide for Uploading Documents for Grounding

1. Install AWS CLI

Run the following command:

brew install awscli

2. Configure AWS CLI

After installation, open Command Prompt (Windows) or Terminal (Mac/Linux) and configure the AWS CLI using your AWS credentials.


Note: For Standard Plan, Use the Write role key for the uploading the documents.

Run the following command:

aws configure

You will be prompted to enter:

  • AWS Access Key ID
  • AWS Secret Access Key
  • Default region name (for example: eu-central-1)
  • Default output format (for example: json, or press Enter to leave it as default)

3. Upload a Grounding Document to AWS S3

To upload a local file to an S3 bucket, use the following command:

aws s3 cp <local-file-path> s3://<bucket-name>/

Example:

aws s3 cp user/…/LA.pdf s3://my-grounding-bucket/

4. Verify File Upload

To confirm that the file was uploaded successfully, list the contents of the S3 bucket:

Note: For Standard plan user again need to configure AWS CLI with read key or you can use Cyberduck for checking the uploaded docs and AWS CLI for uploading.

aws s3 ls s3://<bucket-name>/

If the file appears in the list, the upload was successful.

9. Viewing Grounding Pipelines in SAP AI Launchpad

Once grounding is triggered:

  1. Go to SAP AI Launchpad
  2. Add API Connection
  3. Once loaded, go to: Workspaces → Select the resource Group

    Note: Wait for few minutes until, the resource group is created.


  4. Navigate:
    AI Core Administration → Resource Groups
  5. You will see a new group named:
    {ResourceGroupName}{UnixTimestamp}

  6. Select/Click the resource group, the go to
    Generative AI Hub → Grounding Management → Click on pipeline created.
    This confirms grounding is complete.

    Note: If the pipeline is showing "This data repository is currently empty", then check the destination and s3 bucket.

 

10. Adding Document Grounding to Joule Studio Agents

Developers now consume the resource group.

Steps:

  1. Project Creation: Open Joule Studio → Lobby → Create button → Joule Agent and Skill / Joule Skill
    Give Project Name and Description. Click on the project

  2. Create Joule Agent: Create → Joule Agent

  3. Fill all the necessary details carefully and meaningfully. 
  4. Navigate to Tools Section → Add Tools → Documents


  5. Provide:
    • Tool Name
    • Description
  6. Select one of the available Admin-created Resource Groups

The agent now has grounding enabled.

 

11. Using Joule Studio - Test in Private Environment and Deploy & Launch Joule Agent

  1. Save the Agent.
  2. Make sure Private Environment for testing must be activated: Control Tower → Tenant Configuration (Environments) → Click on Private Environment Active.
  3. Enable Generative AI: Control Tower → Tenant Configuration (Generative AI) → Enable Generative AI.
  4. Enable Generative AI: Control Tower → Tenant Configuration (AI Agent Builder Activation) → Enable Agent Builder.
  5. Joule Agent Window → Test → Select Private → Continue.


  6. Test is woking


  7. Release the Joule Agent


  8. Make a Environment for deploying the Agent: Control Tower → Environments → Create 


  9. Deploy to created environment.


     

  10. Launch: Control Tower → Environments → Select the created environment agent_test_env → More (Joule) → Launch 


    You can use the link as per your preference.


12. Troubleshooting

Common issues:
- Wrong destination credentials
- Missing '/oauth/token'
- Corrupted PDF files in Object Store
- Resource group stuck in Pending

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to reverse the change of the “Time‑Bas...
entry.content:
Reversibility of Changes

Configuration-level changes: Switching the RR key from Time-Based Methods to Method A (Calendar days of financial periods, actual value date) is a configuration adjustment. These changes are not destructive — they can be reversed by restoring the previous method.
Simulation vs. Posting: When you run a simulation/analysis, the system calculates revenue recognition based on the new method but doesn’t post actual accounting entries. This gives you the chance to validate outcomes before committing.
Rollback options: If the business is not satisfied, you can revert the RR key back to the original method. Any postings already made under the new method can be reversed using standard RAR reversal transactions, though this requires careful coordination with Finance to ensure reporting consistency.

Key considerations

Audit trail: Every change in revenue recognition logic should be documented for compliance purposes.
Impact analysis: Switching methods affects period-end reporting, KPIs, and disclosures. Finance and Controlling teams should sign off before production deployment.
Flexibility: SAP RAR is designed to allow method changes, but once postings are made in production, reversals can be administratively heavy. That’s why simulation and parallel testing are critical.

entry.content: The “Clear Incoming Payments” and “Clear Outgoing Payments” apps handle clearing incoming and outgoing payments. When processing partial payments or partial receipts, the “Post on Account” tab is used to record new receivables and payables. However, the problem is, the “Profit Center” on the “Post on Account” tab cannot be specified arbitrarily. Please change the system to allow arbitrary specification.

entry.content: We manage the due dates for receivables and payables using the “Manage Customer Line Items” and “Manage Supplier Line Items” apps. These apps display the “Profit Center” field, but the “Profit Center” value is not populated for receivable and payable records posted within SAP. We request a change to enable viewing the “Profit Center” within the apps.

entry.title: Permit changes to input data during the approval process of Workflow
entry.content: The standard workflow functionality does not permit changes to input data during the approval process. Therefore, if changes are necessary, the application must be rejected and resubmitted. In current operations, approvers sometimes need to modify specific items (such as account codes or tax items) to improve efficiency. We request that approvers be allowed to edit these items.

entry.title: Re: How to remove budget from released network
entry.content:
Options to Remove/Surrender Budget

1) Budget Return to Investment Program
Use transaction IM52 (Change Investment Program Position) or the equivalent Fiori app. Reduce the budget assigned to the WBS element/network. The surrendered budget flows back to the investment program position.

2) Budget Transfer
If you want to move the budget to another WBS/network, use budget transfer functionality (e.g., CJ34 for WBS budget changes). This keeps the total program budget intact but reallocates funds.

3) Budget Supplement/Return
In CJ30 (Change Project Budget), you can post a return entry against the WBS/network. This effectively reduces the available budget for that object while keeping audit trail intact.

4) Controlling Considerations
Ensure that commitments or actuals posted against the network are not exceeding the reduced budget. If actuals already exist, you may need to repost or settle costs before reducing the budget.

Best Practice

  • Perform the change in test/QA system first to confirm the impact on reporting and availability control.
  • Document the budget return/transfer postings for audit compliance.
  • Coordinate with Controlling and Finance teams so that surrendered budget is properly reflected in program reporting.

In short, you cannot delete the released network, but you can return or transfer its budget using IM52/CJ30. This way, the budget is removed from the specific WBS/network while keeping the investment program consistent.

entry.title: File-based journal entry imports
entry.content: The “Post Tax Payables” app does not currently support file-based journal entry imports. To improve operational efficiency, we request this function to be added.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：增强版 New User Experience Switch 全面解析
entry.content:
在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中，SAP 持续推进用户体验（UX）的革新，其中 New User Experience Switch（新版用户体验切换功能） 是帮助企业顺利完成应用替换的重要机制。最新版本（2602）对该功能进行了进一步增强，让业务用户与管理员更容易适应新旧应用的切换进程。 

         本文将带你了解这个增强功能的核心设计、工作方式，以及企业在实际使用中可以获得的价值。

一、为什么需要 New User Experience Switch？

         SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 每 6 个月发布一次新版本，其中会逐步引入新应用（phase-in），同时也可能逐步淘汰旧应用（phase-out）。通常，业务用户有 至少 12 个月 的窗口期来适应新的替代应用，但在实际项目中，这段时间可能转瞬即逝。 

         为避免用户错过迁移时机，SAP 引入 New User Experience Switch，通过界面提示与切换选项来“温和推动”用户尝试并过渡到新应用。

二、New User Experience Switch 的三种提示阶段

         切换功能在应用生命周期的不同阶段会呈现不同的提示样式：

1. Information 信息阶段

  • 告诉用户有新的应用版本正在被引入
  • 展示新应用的优势
  • 鼓励用户尝试
  • 用户可以随时切回旧应用

         这是“最温和”的阶段，旨在减少用户对改变的抵触情绪。

2. Warning 警告阶段

         当旧应用已经被标记为 deprecated（即将淘汰） 时，切换功能会显示黄色的警告信息：

  • 告知旧应用将在未来版本中不再提供
  • 提醒用户尽快完成迁移

3. Alert 紧急阶段

         这是旧应用被移除前的最后一个版本，切换功能会以更醒目的方式提示：

  • 强调迁移的紧迫性
  • 显示明确的移除时间点

         在这个阶段，如果用户仍然停留在旧应用上，会面临无法继续使用的风险。

三、切换功能如何呈现？

         SAP 在 2502 版本引入 New User Experience Switch，在 2508 与 2602 的版本中进一步增强：

  • 旧应用启动时出现提示 popover（气泡提示）
  • Shell Bar（顶端工具栏）中出现 切换按钮
  • 用户可在新旧应用之间自由切换（取决于阶段）

         这些交互方式让用户无需离开当前体验即可了解版本状态并快速做出选择。

四、对业务用户意味着什么？

✔ 更轻松的学习曲线

不再需要由管理员单独告知新旧应用替代关系，系统会自动提示。

✔ 提前感知变化

避免新版本发布后突然发现旧应用“消失”，有效减少工作中断。

✔ 自主试用新应用

在 phase-in 阶段，用户可以自由在新旧体验中切换，加速熟悉度提升。

五、对管理员意味着什么？

管理员可以利用此功能：

  • 提前规划应用替换节奏
  • 知道哪些用户仍在使用旧应用
  • 在企业层面推动统一迁移

这让应用治理更加可控，也能减少升级周期带来的沟通成本。

六、为什么说这个功能“让改变更容易”？

New User Experience Switch 正是帮助企业完成改变的机制：

  • 让改变更加可见
  • 让迁移更加自主
  • 让用户更有掌控感

对于每一个正在推进数字化、云化的企业来说，这种设计都能有效降低内部阻力。

结语：更智慧、更温和的用户体验演进机制

         New User Experience Switch 的增强不仅让用户更轻松地迈向 SAP Fiori 的现代化体验，也让管理员和企业能够更从容、可控地管理应用生命周期。

         随着 SAP 持续对 UX 的改进（例如 2602 版本中的 AI 辅助、增强的 Launchpad、手机端优化等），未来企业的 SAP 使用体验将更加智能、一致、顺滑。 

         如果你正在规划 S/4HANA Cloud 的上云旅程或准备迎接新版本，这项功能值得你重点关注。

 

参考文档：SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – New User Experie... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: change selection screen variable for KE30 report. ECC
entry.content:
Steps to Add a Variable for KE30

1) Change Report
Go to GR22 (Change Report) or GRR2 (Change Report Group). Identify the report or report group that KE30 is executing.

2) Define the Variable in the Library
Transaction: GRV1 (Create Variable) or GRV2 (Change Variable). Select the library used by your KE30 report (e.g., 1VK, 8A, etc.). Create a new variable for the characteristic (e.g., Company Code, Profit Center, Fiscal Year). Specify the variable type (single value, interval, set, etc.) and whether it should appear on the selection screen.

3) Assign the Variable to the Report
In GR22, edit the report layout. For the row/column where you want dynamic input, assign the variable instead of a fixed value. Save the report.

4) Execution in KE30
When you run KE30, the system will now prompt for the variable on the selection screen. The variable values entered by the user will drive the report output.

Important Notes

  • Variables are library-specific. If your KE30 report uses multiple libraries, ensure the variable is created in the correct one.
  • You can control whether the variable is mandatory, optional, or defaulted.
  • Always transport the variable definition and report changes through your landscape (DEV → QA → PROD).

entry.title: Re: Error in vk11
entry.content:
The error message “Enter a valid value” in VK11 usually isn’t about the material itself being “wrong,” but rather about the condition record input not matching what the system expects. Here are the most common causes:

Why the Error Appears

Incorrect Key Combination:
Each condition type (e.g., PR00 for price, K004 for material discount) has a defined access sequence. If you’re entering a material where the condition type doesn’t expect it, the system rejects it.

Material Master Issues:
Material may not exist in the sales organization/distribution channel you’re working in. Material might be flagged for deletion or not extended to the relevant sales area.

Organizational Data Mismatch:
Wrong sales org, distribution channel, or division entered. Material not assigned to the plant or sales org you’re using.

Condition Table Setup:
If the condition table for that condition type doesn’t include “Material” as a key field, you can’t enter it in VK11.

Formatting/Validity:
Sometimes the error is triggered by typos, leading zeros, or entering a material number without the correct format (e.g., missing leading zeros if the material number is numeric).

How to Troubleshoot

Check Material Master (MM03)
Verify the material exists and is extended to the correct sales org/distribution channel. Confirm it’s not flagged for deletion.

Check Condition Type (V/06)
Look at the access sequence and condition table. Confirm “Material” is a valid key field for that condition type.

Check Organizational Data in VK11
Make sure you’re entering the right sales org/distribution channel/division combination.

Try Another Material
If another material works, the issue is likely with the master data setup of the original material.

entry.title: Re: Receive Events from Event Mesh of Integration Suite to CAP Node.js Applications
entry.content:
Hi Andrea, good question! Based on your scenario, I would recommend upgrading to Advanced Event Mesh - it's designed exactly for situations like yours. 

Advanced Event Mesh offers different service classes with higher connection limits. For 400 connections, you'd simply select the Broker 1K class, which supports up to 1,000 concurrent client connections. 

Unlike the standalone Event Mesh with its 200-connection hard limit, a single Advanced Event Mesh instance with Broker 1K handles all 400 connections. Your CAP app and all iFlows can connect to one broker, simplifying your architecture significantly.

entry.title: Re: Business has a custom line item numbering for Sales D...
entry.content:
Hello @bigflatfoot 

First, challenge the requirement. There is all but zero business value in it. It is not SAP standard. It can't be implemented within clean core. The requirement should have been rejected all together.

Second, explain that the custom item numbering can't be implemented when items are created in VA01 or VA02. If it can be implemented at all, the items need to be renumbered when a sales order is saved.

Third, you can try to renumber the items with USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE enhancement, include MV45AFZZ. I'm not sure if it's going to work, but it's the only way I can see.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to make a one-time annual accrual on top of the d...
entry.content:
Hi @MarioneDelaCruz ,

If you normally accrue 0.05 days per day and want to add an extra 1 day every October 1st, you can set up your accrual rule like this:

If Today = October 1st → Post 1.05 days

Else→ Post 0.05 days

you can use Createdate() to compare against October1st.

Regards,

VR

entry.title: Setup Actions and Events for integration in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
This blog explains how to do the setup for Actions for a Sales Order scenario and how to consume this in SAP Build Process Automation. It also has reference for business events setup.

 

Event Setup

If you are using event-based integration for starting the process using Event Triggers please refer to the links for the setup S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition ( Configure SAP Enterprise Messaging for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2020 via Service Key ) and S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition ( Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation).  Here is a detailed link describing how to use Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation : Manage Credit Memo Request

 

Actions Project Setup

“Actions” in SAP Build Process Automation connects processes to external systems, whether SAP or non-SAP. This is crucial for automating or expanding business processes for various LoB processes such as S/4HANA, Ariba, and SuccessFactors. With SAP Build Process Automation, these extensions can be easily built, and through actions, connections can be made to S/4HANA, Ariba, or other SAP LoB systems for GET, POST, PATCH, and other types of calls.

In this blog we will create an action project based on Sales Order API with the following steps.

  • Step 1 - Set Up Connection to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition
  • Step 2 - Set up Communication Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Edition
  • Step 3 - Add destination in SAP Build
  • Step 4 - Create Action Project in SAP Build
  • Step 5 - Test the Action Project
  • Step 6 - Release and Publish the Action Project.

Step 1 – Set Up Connection to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition

1)  Enable APIs in SAP Gateway Client

Please follow this blog post to enable the API for the service API_SALES_ORDER_SRV.

2)    Configure Cloud Connector

Please follow this blog post steps to configure the cloud connector.

  • Configure Destination for SAP Build Process Automation
  • After the Cloud Connector is configured, you should be able to see the Cloud Connector in your SAP BTP Subaccount under Connectivity -> Cloud Connectors.
  • Now let’s create destination for Sales Order API from BTP Cockpit->  Subaccount -> Destinations

  • Make sure to provide the following additional properties while creating the destination:

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

sap.processautomation.enabled = true

sap-client=<ClientID>

  • Click on Check Connection and test that the connection is successful.

Step 2: Set up Communication Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

Prerequisite: please make sure you should have an administrator access for SAP S/4HANA system.

For the following setup, we will use communication scenario for Sales Order Integration (SAP_COM_0109).

Optional: You can learn more about communication scenarios here.

  1. Create Communication User

1.1 Login to SAP S/4HANA Cloud System and select 'Communication Management' group:

1.2 Select ‘Maintain Communication Users’ App and click 'New':

1.3 Afterwards, go ahead and provide User Name, Description & Password and click 'Create':

1.4 Once you click 'Create', the Password Status becomes Productive. Now click 'Save': 

Great! The Communication User is now successfully created.

Next up, Communication System and Communication Arrangement need to be created.

2. Create Communication System:

2.1 Login to SAP S/4HANA Cloud System, select 'Communication Management' group and Click 'Communication Systems' app (similar to step 1.1).

2.2 Here you would see the list of already existing Communication Systems if any. Click 'New' to create a new Communication System:

2.3 Enter a System ID and System Name and click 'Create':

2.4 Provide the Host Name and HTTPS Port of the system you want to connect to.

We will use a hypothetical name and port here.

Click 'Add' button for inbound communication to add the user for authenticating inbound request:

Note: Here we are using inbound service hence the 'Logical System' is optional but when we make use of outbound service then 'Logical System' plays an important role and is mandatory to be filled.

2.5 Select the Communication User (from value help) which we had just created. The assigned Communication User will now automatically appear in Username field. Click 'Save'.

2.6 Click 'Save' and the Communication System is created:

Now we have successfully created a Communication System with a Communication User assigned to it.

3. Create Communication arrangement based on the relevant Communication Scenario:

3.1 Login to SAP S/4HANA Cloud System, select 'Communication Management' group and Click ‘Communication Arrangement’ app (like step 2.1).

3.2 Once you are within the 'Communication Arrangements' application you would see the existing Communication Arrangements if any. Click 'New':

3.3 Here select the relevant 'Communication Scenario' (from value help) based on the API you would want to access. Like in our example we will use Sales Order Integration (SAP_COM_0109) for this case and click 'Create':

 

Note: You would be able find relevant Communication Scenarios in the API documentation from SAP API Business Hub.

3.4 Select the 'Communication System' (from value help) which we have just created. The assigned Communication User will now automatically appear in User Name field. Click 'Save':

The Communication Arrangement is now created successfully.

4. Configure Destination for SAP Build Process Automation

We will now create the destination for Sales Order APIs. 

First, go to SAP BTP Cockpit -> Subaccount -> Destinations. You will see the available destination listed. Make sure that the additional properties are as below:

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

sap.processautomation.enabled = true

sap-client=<ClientID>

In this example, we are using the destination DCOM_Sales_Order as a reference only.

And finally, click on Check Connection to test it.

The connection should indicate OK:

Step 3: Add destination in SAP Build

Next, to use this destination, we will connect it to SAP Build Process Automation. From the SAP Build lobby, click on Control Tower.

Click on Destinations, to configure the destination with SAP Build:

Look for the Destination name from the previous step in the search bar and click on the 3 dots: 

Afterwards, we specify the Environment in which the destination will be configured to. Choose any environment  (here the screenshot shows Public) and Add Destination.

Environments refer to the different stages or platforms where the automated processes will run, such as development, testing, and production. Each environment may have different sets of configurations and requirements.

 

 

Step 4: Create Action Project in SAP Build

  1. Please download the pre-configured Action Project by clicking here. You will download the mtar. file from GitHub to your device as below:

2. Now head on over to the SAP Build Lobby. Click on the Import function:

3. Browse the imported file from your machine to import it. 

 

4. Great, now that you have imported the Action project. Let's review it. Go to Actions function on the left-hand side. Then, double click on the Sales Order project.

5. This will open the Actions Editor function in a new tab with the Action project downloaded. As you can see there are 3 APIs inside:

Step 5 - Test the Action Project

It is now time to test the APIs. Let's start testing starting with the GET API: Reads the header of a sales order. Click on it:

 

After clicking on the configured destination, click on the Test tab and provide a valid SalesOrder number. Click on the Test button:

 

Test response reading the header of the relevant Sales Order will be provided as the following:

 

Now, from the Actions tab, and let's test the other GET API: Reads all items of a Sales Order by following the same steps as before:

 

Check Response:

 

Great job! We have now configured and tested the actions you downloaded successfully for our exercise.

Step 6 - Release and Publish the Action Project

 

You will now release the action project to create version(s) and then publish that version in the action repository. It is then these published actions that can be used in different processes and applications to connect to external systems.

To release a version of the action project, click Release from top-right corner.

 

In the release popup, enter the Release Notes of your choice and click Release.

If you have Publish to Library button instead of Release, please directly Publish as prescribed in the next step.

 

Once the action project is released, you can then publish the action by clicking Publish from top-right corner and confirm Publish. Wait for confirmation that it is published.

 

With this you have successfully completed creating, configuring, releasing and publishing of action project.

Now you can use these published actions in any SAP Build Process Automation project.
